name: Baztandarrak – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Baztandarrak en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The Baztandarrak is a trail race which has the particularity of changing course almost every year. Therefore, here I offer you the route of the 2022 edition. It is a demanding loop of about 22 kilometers which includes 1,600 meters of elevation gain.

The event generally takes place during the second half of July and starts from the village of Bidarray. Nestled at the foot of the magnificent Iparla ridges, Bidarray offers spectacular views of the surrounding mountains. Atypical village of the Basque Country, there reigns a peaceful and welcoming atmosphere.

From the first meters the route rises uphill following part of the GR 10. After three kilometers a switchover takes place and you take a rolling descent to the river below. Enjoy the path winding through a stunning forest as the path rises sharply thereafter.

A steep climb guides you to the edge of the Espalza pass. Then you take a magnificent path that runs along impressive cliffs. Savor the immensity of the panoramas that are gradually revealed. Take the opportunity to catch your breath because two nice portions await you.

At first you climb on the peak of Iguzki, from which you see your ultimate ascent: the Artzamendi. These two summits give you magnificent 360-degree panoramas over the valleys of the Basque Country.

From the last summit, there is practically only a long downhill portion to the village of Bidarray. Let yourself embark on its pleasant paths while admiring the view it provides. After crossing the river one last time, a very short last climb awaits you before sliding down to the village on a stretch of road.

The Baztandarrak association also offers another race of about 42 kilometers.

For more information on the event:
https://www.baztandarrak.fr/
region_intro: Jogging routes Bidarray offer a diverse landscape for runners, situated in the Northern Basque Country at the foot of the Iparla ridges. The region features significant mountains like Artzamendi and Baïgura, providing varied elevation for challenging runs. The Nive River flows through the area, offering flatter paths along its banks, while extensive networks of trails wind through lush forests and green countryside. This combination of mountainous terrain, river valleys, and woodlands creates a dynamic environment for running. Best jogging routes around Bidarray The most popular jogging route is Baztandarrak – running in the Basque Country, a 14.2 miles (22.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes through the Basque Countryside. Another top favourite among local runners is From Gorramendi to Iparla – running in the Basque Country, a difficult 15.1 miles (24.4 km) path. This running route traverses mountainous terrain, including the Iparla ridges, with substantial climbs. Local runners also love the Bidarray – Pic d'Iparla loop from Bidarray-Pont-Noblia, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail leading through the challenging Pic d'Iparla area, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Jogging around Bidarray is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and lush forests. The extensive trail network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult mountain running routes. The routes in Bidarray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 5000 runners have used komoot to explore Bidarray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Bidarray?
A: Bidarray offers a diverse network of over 20 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. While most are challenging, there are a few moderate options available for those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails around Bidarray?
A: The terrain around Bidarray is quite varied, ranging from mountainous paths with significant elevation changes, especially towards the Iparla ridges and peaks like Artzamendi and Baïgura, to flatter, more bucolic routes along the Nive River. You'll encounter a mix of natural, unpaved tracks, forest trails, and some asphalt sections, all set within lush forests and green countryside.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Bidarray?
A: While the majority of routes in Bidarray are classified as difficult due to the mountainous terrain and elevation gains, there are a couple of moderate options available. These might include paths along the Nive River, which offer a gentler gradient and a more relaxed running experience compared to the challenging mountain trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Bidarray?
A: The running routes in Bidarray are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Runners frequently praise the stunning panoramic views, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the immersive experience of running through the Northern Basque Country's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there circular running paths in Bidarray?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Bidarray are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35359309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bidarray – Pic d'Iparla loop from Bidarray-Pont-Noblia&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging circuit through the Pic d'Iparla area.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Bidarray?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Bidarray region, especially on natural paths through forests and mountains. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging options in Bidarray?
A: For families or those looking for less strenuous runs, the flatter paths along the Nive River offer more accessible options. While specific 'easy' routes are limited, these riverside trails provide a scenic and manageable environment for a family jog. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile on komoot before heading out.

Q: What are some scenic running routes with great views?
A: Bidarray is renowned for its scenic beauty. Routes that traverse the Iparla ridges or ascend peaks like Artzamendi and Baïgura offer breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and the Basque countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046251706/from-gorramendi-to-iparla-running-in-the-basque-country" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Gorramendi to Iparla – running in the Basque Country&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, provides substantial climbs with rewarding vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights I can see while running?
A: Yes, the Bidarray region is rich in history and culture. You might encounter the Roman Noblia bridge, the 17th-century Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, or even paths that were historically part of the Way of Saint-Jacques. The nearby village of Ainhoa, a 'Most Beautiful Village of France', is also accessible via the GR10 trail. Consider exploring highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roland's Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2562367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mehatzeko Lepoa Stone Circle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trails in Bidarray?
A: Bidarray, being a village, typically offers public parking areas, often near the village center or specific trailheads. For routes starting from Bidarray-Pont-Noblia, you can usually find parking nearby. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for specific parking information, as options can vary.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Bidarray?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running in Bidarray, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon runs are preferable. Winter running is possible but requires appropriate gear for potentially colder and wetter conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any longer, challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. Bidarray is a paradise for experienced trail runners seeking a challenge. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041855954/baztandarrak-running-in-the-basque-country" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baztandarrak – running in the Basque Country&lt;/a&gt; (over 22 km with significant elevation) or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40998657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artzamendi – Mount Mondarrain loop from Bidarray-Pont-Noblia&lt;/a&gt; (over 33 km) offer substantial distances and considerable climbs through demanding mountainous terrain.
tips: Magnificent trail that winds along superb cliffs. It reveals superb panoramas as you progress.
The peak of Iguzki is located at 842 meters above sea level and offers an incredible panoramic view of the surrounding mountains.

Mount Artzamendi is reached by a short but particularly demanding climb. From Laxia, the climb is 5.6 kilometers at an average of 11% with passages at 19%. At the top, the panorama of the Basque Country extends practically to the Atlantic coast.

Perched on its rocky promontory, the small village of Bidarray offers magnificent views of the mountains that surround it. Nestled at the foot of the Iparla ridges, Bidarray is on the GR 10 path and offers plenty of supplies.
